extracted.raw_summary: Servier entered into a definitive merger agreement to acquire Day One Biopharmaceuticals via a cash tender offer, followed by a second-step merger for any remaining shares at the same price

source_text:
EVENT

Servier entered into a definitive merger agreement to acquire Day One Biopharmaceuticals via a cash tender offer, followed by a second-step merger for any remaining shares at the same price.
KEY FIGURES

$21.50 per share in cash
Total equity value of approximately $2.5 billion
~68% premium over closing price on March 5, 2026
~86% premium over one-month VWAP as of March 5, 2026
Servier FY 2024/25 revenues of €6.9 billion
MARKET IMPACT

Day One shareholders would receive $21.50 per share in cash. Day One's Board of Directors recommends shareholders tender their shares. Servier expects to fund the transaction through existing cash and investments. The acquisition expands Servier's rare oncology portfolio, particularly in pediatric low-grade glioma.
FORWARD GUIDANCE

The transaction is expected to close in Q2 2026. The tender offer has not yet commenced; Servier and the Purchaser will file tender offer materials on Schedule TO with the SEC upon commencement.
DEAL RELEVANCE

Closing conditions include tender of at least a majority of Day One's outstanding shares and receipt of U.S. antitrust clearance. Upon successful completion of the tender offer, Servier will acquire remaining shares via a second-step merger at the same $21.50 per share price. Day One's Board recommends shareholders tender their shares.
RISKS FLAGGED

Uncertainty as to timing of the offer and subsequent merger
Uncertainty as to how many stockholders will tender their shares
Risk of competing offers or acquisition proposals
Possibility that various closing conditions may not be satisfied or waived
Effects of disruption from the transaction on Day One's business and relationships with employees, suppliers, and business partners
Risk that stockholder litigation in connection with the offer or merger may result in significant costs
